title: Wet-lab identification of Leishmania donovani antigens detected by human IgG1
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"nfbh","text":"This protocol describes a wet-lab method to identify Leishmania proteins bands recognized by human IgG1. The cytosolic proteins obtained by cell lysis are initially separated by tricine gel electrophoresis using a long (14 cm) apparatus (SG-200, CBS Scientific, USA). Upon completion of the gel electrophoresis, the proteins are transferred onto a nitrocellulose (NC) membrane (10600046, GE Healthcare Life Sciences, UK) using a semi-dry electroblotting transfer system (1703940, Bio-Rad, UK). The NC membrane is sliced into several thin (4mm) strips and each of the strip is immunoassayed with individual sera from Indian patients with different clinical status of visceral leishmaniasis - VL (active VL, relapse, endemic healthy controls and non-endemic healthy controls) using an HRP-conjugated anti-human IgG1 secondary antibody (ab99774, Abcam, Cambridge, UK)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"6r5k2","text":"Acrylamide and bisacrylamide are highly neurotoxic. When handling these chemicals, wear gloves, use a pipetting aid and always work under the hood","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"9kkaj","text":"Tris, tricine, SDS, HCl, acrylamide, bisacrylamide, amonium persulphate, TEMED, dH2O,  glycerol, Coomassie blue G-250, PBS,  acetic acid, non-fat milk powder, Tween 20, mouse anti-human IgG1-HRP monoclonal antibody, SigmaFast DAB tablets","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Prepare 10x solutions of cathode buffer (Tris 1M, Tricine 1M, SDS 1%, pH ~8,25) and anode buffer (Tris 1M, HCl 0.225M, pH 8.9)
Prepare an acrylamide-bisacrylamide (AB-3) stock solution by dissolving 48 g of acrylamide and 1.5g of bisacrylamide (each twice-crystallized; Serva) in 100 ml of water
Prepare a 3x gel buffer solution (Tris 3M, HCl 1M, SDS 0,3%, pH 8,45)
Upon gel completion, disassemble the gel apparatus and remove the stacking gel
Cover the transfer system with the lid

Upon completion of the protein transfer, block the membrane with blocking buffer (PBS + 3% w/v non-fat milk powder overnight at 4°C followed by three 5 minute washes in PBST (PBS + 0,05% Tween 20) and one wash of PBST + 0.02% w/v sodium azide
Add enough mouse anti-human IgG1-HRP (ab99774, Abcam, UK) diluted 1:1000 in blocking buffer to cover the strips
To prepare 20ml of a 10% resolving gel solution, mix 4ml of AB-3, 6.66ml of 3x gel buffer solution and 9.33ml of dH 2 O.
Store the gel overnight with a dH 2 O overlay at 4 o C. Cover the top of the gel with a plastic film to minimise evaporation
Pour the 10% resolving gel solution into the gel apparatus with a pipette, leaving a space of about the height of the comb that will be used + 1,5cm (where the 4% stacking gel will be later casted)
Add the polymerising agents: 100 µl of 10% amonium persulphate (APS) and 10 µl of TEMED
Store the gel at 4 o C until complete solidification of the stacking gel
Directly insert the comb (avoid the formation of air bubbles underneath the comb)
Pour the 4% stacking gel solution into the gel apparatus with a pippete (onto the top of the resolving gel, now solidified)
Add the polymerising agents: 67.5 µl of 10% APS solutions and 6.75 µl of TEMED

On the following day, carefully pour off the dH 2 O on the top of the (now solidified resolving gel) and prepare about 4 ml of a 4% stacking gel solution by mixing 0.33ml of AB-3, 1ml of gel buffer 3x and 2,67ml of dH 2 O
Mount the gel electrophoresis apparatus and fill the cathode and anode tanks with 1x cathode and anode buffer, respectively

Prepare the samples to be loaded into the wells by mixing 3 parts of L. donovani lysate with 1 part of non-reducing sample buffer (2% SDS (wt/vol), 30% glycerol (wt/vol), 0.05% Coomassie blue G-250 (Serva), 150 mM Tris/HCl (pH 7.0). The final L. donovani concentration should be 4 µg/mm lane. Depending on the initial concentration of the L. donovani lysate, use PBS to dilute it to a final total protein load of 4 µg/mm lane
Carefully rinse the wells multiple times with dH 2 0.
Carefully and slowly remove the comb in order to avoid damaging to the stacking gel
Load the wells with the samples and protein molecular weight marker (1610374, Bio-Rad, UK)
Run the gel at 30V constant for 55min, then 120V constant for a total of 5h
Squeeze out air bubbles by gently rolling any cylindrical object (e.g. a Falcon tube) against the top layer of blotting paper
Add four layers of blotting paper previously soaked with blotting buffer on the top of the gel. Add more blotting buffer if necessary. Do not flood the system
Carefully add the gel on the top of the NC membrane. Ensure full contact between gel and NC membrane along the whole surface
Add the NC membrane (10600046, GE Healthcare Life Sciences, UK) on the top of the soaked blotting paper
Soak three layers of blotting paper (Whatman GB005) with blotting buffer (300 mM Tris, 100 mM acetic acid (pH 8.6). Do not flood the semi-dry system (1703940, Bio-Rad, UK)
Upon completion of the transfer, air dry the membranes. Place heavy objects onto the four corners of the membrane, to avoid curling.
Run for 120 minutes at constant 60 mA
Wash the strips with PBST six times for 10 minutes each
Incubate at rocking for 1h at room temperature (RT)
Add enough serum samples diluted 1:400 in blocking buffer to cover the strips
Pre-wet the individual strips with PBS and place them in wells of an 8-well immunotray (octa-101, Pateof, Denmark)
Number the strips sequentially (including the MWM). Store in airtight containers at 4 o C until use.
Tape the top of the membrane (for identification, see step below) and slice the blocked NC membrane into 4 mm strips using a paper trimmer (Cathedral Products, UK)
To reveal the protein bands, dissolve 1 tablet pair of SigmaFast DAB (D0426, Sigma-Aldrich) in 15ml of dH 2 O immediately prior to use.
Wash the strips with PBST six times for 10 minutes each
Incubate at rocking for 1h at RT
Wash thoroughly with water
Add enough volume to cover the strips and incubate for 4 minutes in darkness
Air-dry the strips with heavy objects on top and bottom to avoid curling.
creator: Bruno Hinckel
